core.label: JavaScript Fundamentals
core.description: Syntax, types, operators, and basic program structure.

topics.label: Variables (let, const, var)
topics.description: Declare variables with let and const. Understand var hoisting and why const is preferred by default.

topics.label: Data Types
topics.description: string, number, bigint, boolean, null, undefined, symbol, and object. JS is dynamically typed.

topics.label: Operators & Type Coercion
topics.description: Arithmetic, comparison (=== vs ==), logical, and nullish coalescing (??). Type coercion is JS's biggest gotcha.

topics.label: Control Flow
topics.description: if/else, switch, for, while, for...of, for...in, break, continue. Standard flow control.

topics.label: Strings
topics.description: Template literals, string methods, and Unicode. Template literals with backticks are the modern standard.

topics.label: Arrays
topics.description: push, pop, map, filter, reduce, find, includes, slice, splice. Arrays are the most-used data structure.

topics.label: Objects
topics.description: Object literals, property access, computed keys, shorthand, and destructuring. Objects are JS's core data container.

topics.label: Destructuring & Spread
topics.description: Extract values from arrays/objects and spread elements. Essential for clean modern JS code.

topics.label: Map, Set, WeakMap, WeakSet
topics.description: Keyed collections beyond plain objects. Map allows any key type; Set stores unique values.

topics.label: JSON
topics.description: Parse and stringify JSON. JSON is the universal format for data exchange.

core.label: Functions & Scope
core.description: Function declarations, closures, and execution context.

topics.label: Function Declarations & Expressions
topics.description: Named functions, anonymous functions, and hoisting differences. Functions are first-class citizens in JS.

topics.label: Arrow Functions
topics.description: Concise syntax with => and lexical this binding. Arrow functions don't have their own this or arguments.

topics.label: Parameters & Defaults
topics.description: Default parameters, rest parameters (...args), and the arguments object.

topics.label: Closures
topics.description: Functions that capture their enclosing scope. Closures are fundamental to JS patterns like modules and callbacks.

topics.label: this Keyword
topics.description: Understand this in different contexts — global, method, constructor, arrow, and with call/apply/bind.

topics.label: Higher-Order Functions
topics.description: Functions that take or return functions — map, filter, reduce, sort callbacks. Core functional pattern.

topics.label: IIFE & Module Pattern
topics.description: Immediately Invoked Function Expressions and the revealing module pattern. Historical but still seen in codebases.

topics.label: Scope & Hoisting
topics.description: Block scope (let/const), function scope (var), and temporal dead zone. Scope bugs are common for beginners.

core.label: Asynchronous JavaScript
core.description: Callbacks, promises, async/await, and the event loop.

topics.label: The Event Loop
topics.description: Understand the call stack, task queue, and microtask queue. The event loop explains all async behavior in JS.

topics.label: Callbacks
topics.description: Pass functions as arguments for async completion. Callbacks are the original async pattern but lead to callback hell.

topics.label: Promises
topics.description: Represent eventual values with Promise, then, catch, finally. Promises chain async operations cleanly.

topics.label: Promise Combinators
topics.description: Promise.all, allSettled, race, and any. Combine multiple promises for parallel async operations.

topics.label: async/await
topics.description: Write async code that reads like synchronous code. async/await is syntactic sugar over promises.

topics.label: Error Handling in Async
topics.description: try/catch with async/await and .catch() with promises. Unhandled rejections crash Node.js processes.

topics.label: Fetch API
topics.description: Make HTTP requests with fetch(). The modern replacement for XMLHttpRequest.

topics.label: Iterators & Generators
topics.description: Custom iterables with Symbol.iterator and generator functions with yield. Power async iteration patterns.

core.label: OOP & Prototypes
core.description: Classes, prototypes, inheritance, and object patterns.

topics.label: Prototypal Inheritance
topics.description: Understand __proto__, Object.create, and the prototype chain. JS inheritance is prototype-based, not class-based.

topics.label: Classes
topics.description: ES6 class syntax with constructor, methods, static, and private fields (#). Classes are syntactic sugar over prototypes.

topics.label: Inheritance with extends
topics.description: Extend classes, call super(), and override methods. Class inheritance works through the prototype chain.

topics.label: Getters & Setters
topics.description: Define computed properties with get/set. Useful for validation and derived values.

topics.label: Symbols
topics.description: Unique identifiers for object properties. Symbols enable well-known protocols like Symbol.iterator.

topics.label: Proxy & Reflect
topics.description: Intercept object operations with Proxy. Used in frameworks for reactivity and validation.

topics.label: Mixins & Composition
topics.description: Compose behavior from multiple sources instead of deep inheritance. Preferred over class hierarchies.

topics.label: Object.freeze, seal, assign
topics.description: Control object mutability and shallow copy. Important for immutable state patterns.

core.label: Modules & Tooling
core.description: ES modules, package management, and development tools.

topics.label: ES Modules
topics.description: import/export syntax for code organization. ES modules are the standard module system for JavaScript.

topics.label: CommonJS (require)
topics.description: Node.js module system with require/module.exports. Still widely used in Node.js codebases.

topics.label: npm & package.json
topics.description: Install dependencies, manage scripts, and understand semver. npm is the default JS package manager.

topics.label: Bundlers (Vite, webpack)
topics.description: Bundle modules for the browser. Vite is the modern default; webpack is still everywhere.

topics.label: Linting with ESLint
topics.description: Catch bugs and enforce style with ESLint. ESLint is the standard JS linter.

topics.label: Prettier
topics.description: Opinionated code formatter. Prettier ends style debates and integrates with ESLint.

topics.label: Debugging in DevTools
topics.description: Chrome DevTools debugger — breakpoints, network tab, console, and profiler.

topics.label: TypeScript Basics
topics.description: Add static types to JavaScript with TypeScript. TS catches bugs at compile time and improves IDE support.

core.label: Error Handling & DOM
core.description: Exception handling, the DOM API, and browser events.

topics.label: try/catch/finally
topics.description: Handle runtime errors and throw custom errors. Proper error handling prevents silent failures.

topics.label: Custom Errors
topics.description: Extend Error class for domain-specific errors. Custom errors make debugging and error handling clearer.

topics.label: DOM Selection & Manipulation
topics.description: querySelector, createElement, textContent, classList, and attribute methods. The DOM is how JS interacts with HTML.

topics.label: Events & Event Delegation
topics.description: addEventListener, event bubbling, capturing, and delegation. Delegation handles events on dynamic content.

topics.label: Local Storage & Session Storage
topics.description: Persist data in the browser with localStorage and sessionStorage. Simple key-value storage for client state.

topics.label: Regular Expressions
topics.description: Pattern matching with RegExp. Used for validation, parsing, and search/replace.

topics.label: Timers & requestAnimationFrame
topics.description: setTimeout, setInterval, and requestAnimationFrame for scheduling. Used in animations and polling.

topics.label: Web APIs Overview
topics.description: Intersection Observer, Clipboard, Geolocation, Notifications, and more. The browser provides a rich API surface.

core.label: Testing & Best Practices
core.description: Testing frameworks, code quality, and modern JS patterns.

topics.label: Jest / Vitest
topics.description: Write unit tests with assertions, mocks, and snapshots. Vitest is the modern alternative with Vite compatibility.

topics.label: Testing Library
topics.description: Test DOM components by user behavior, not implementation details. Works with React, Vue, and vanilla JS.

topics.label: E2E Testing (Playwright)
topics.description: End-to-end browser testing with Playwright. Tests real user flows across browsers.

topics.label: Immutability Patterns
topics.description: Avoid mutation with spread, Object.freeze, and immutable updates. Immutability prevents subtle bugs.

topics.label: Error Boundaries & Defensive Coding
topics.description: Validate inputs, handle edge cases, and fail gracefully. Defensive code is reliable code.

topics.label: Performance Basics
topics.description: Debouncing, throttling, lazy loading, and avoiding layout thrashing. Know the common performance pitfalls.

topics.label: Security Basics (XSS, CSRF)
topics.description: Prevent cross-site scripting and request forgery. Sanitize user input and use Content Security Policy.

topics.label: Modern JS Features (ES2020+)
topics.description: Optional chaining (?.), nullish coalescing (??), at(), structuredClone, and top-level await.
